<!-- Verified: 2026-07-09 · Derived from reference/knowledge/ — regenerated by /seo --research-dev-only.
     Do not hand-edit for content drift; edit the knowledge entries and re-run --research-dev-only. -->

# SEO audit checklist — the operational spine

The audit walks this file **top to bottom**. It is ordered by impact: indexation integrity first
(the changes that can remove a site from Google), then crawlability, rendering, on-page, media,
structured data, social, i18n, local, performance, content, and answer-engine optimization.

**Each check is self-sufficient to run** from its inline probe and fix recipe. The `Depth:` pointer
is where `reference/knowledge/` carries the dated evidence, the source URL, and the edge cases -
read it when a check needs a threshold's provenance or a full recipe. Every threshold here is
mirrored, dated, in a knowledge entry; the number is restated inline only so the check is operable.

**How to read a check:**
`ID — Name · Severity floor · Fix tier` then **Failure / Probe / Fix / Verify**.
Severity floors: Critical / High / Medium / Low / Info (see `reference/severity.md`).
Fix tiers (see the SKILL's Safety spine): **A** = safe, auto-apply · **B** = generated text, propose
then apply, grounded-only · **C** = de-index class, **never silent**, confirm batch.

Run every check against every in-scope template class (or once site-wide where noted). A check that
is genuinely N/A is a **one-line documented skip**, never a silent omission. Assess against
**rendered** output; if only source is available, mark render-dependent findings
"source-level, unverified". Respect `claude/seo/decisions.md` — skip suppressed choices, never
re-propose a rejected fix. Phase 0 (stack detection) resolves the generator, head-injection
mechanism, config surfaces, and base-URL config that every fix below relies on — SEO signals are
*emitted*, not typed as literal tags, so probe the rendered output, not the source template.
*Depth:* `knowledge/foundations/stack-signals.md`, `knowledge/foundations/thresholds.md`

**Verification tooling (advisory, not in-repo):** Field, coverage, and markup truth lives in tools
the audit *reads* but cannot apply — Google Search Console (URL Inspection live-vs-cached,
Page-indexing, Sitemaps, robots.txt reports, and the June-2026 impressions-only Generative-AI
performance reports for Search AI Overviews/AI Mode and Discover), PageSpeed Insights (CrUX field vs Lighthouse lab), the
Rich Results Test (Google rich-result *eligibility*) vs the Schema Markup Validator (generic
schema.org validity), and Bing Webmaster Tools (URL Inspection, Site Scan, IndexNow, the 2026-beta
AI-performance/GEO report). Route retired checks to their live replacements — the standalone
robots.txt Tester and the Mobile-Friendly Test / Mobile Usability report are gone; never recommend a
dead URL. Treat every one of these as advisory evidence and never gate a verdict on a surface the
audit cannot fetch. *Depth:* `knowledge/measurement-tooling/gsc-verify-reports.md`,
`knowledge/measurement-tooling/gsc-generative-ai-performance-reports.md`,
`knowledge/measurement-tooling/pagespeed-field-vs-lab.md`,
`knowledge/measurement-tooling/structured-data-validators.md`,
`knowledge/measurement-tooling/bing-webmaster-tools.md`,
`knowledge/measurement-tooling/retired-seo-tools.md`

---

## Phase X — Indexation integrity (de-index class · always Tier C · checked FIRST)

The most damaging, most silent SEO bugs. A wrong value here removes pages from the index for weeks.
**None of these auto-apply** — every fix is a before → after entry in the confirm batch (or `--force`
with the warning). Where the "correct" value is not unambiguous from Phase 0, downgrade to flag-only.

### X1 — Canonical integrity · Critical · C
- **Failure:** A page has no `rel="canonical"`, or its canonical points to a non-200 / redirected /
  `noindex` / different page, or conflicting canonicals exist, or a wrong base-URL config makes every
  page canonicalize off-site. Splits or destroys ranking signals; a bad base URL de-indexes the whole
  site. (Note: `rel="canonical"` is a strong *hint* Google can override — it clusters duplicates and
  may pick its own; a clean self-canonical is how you steer that choice.)
- **Probe:** In each rendered `<head>` find exactly one `<link rel="canonical">`; resolve its `href`
  to a live `200`; confirm it is absolute and self-referencing on canonical-original pages; verify the
  Phase-0 base-URL config is correct. The canonical is honored only as one absolute-URL annotation in
  `<head>` (or an HTTP `Link` header for non-HTML like PDFs) — in the body, duplicated, or as a
  fragment it is discarded.
- **Fix:** **Adding** a self-canonical to a canonical-original page is Tier **A**. **Retargeting** an
  existing canonical, or a host/base-URL change, is Tier **C** — confirm. Inject via the resolved head
  mechanism using the base-URL config. Cross-domain canonicals are technically honored but no longer
  recommended for syndicated content — prefer `noindex` on the partner.
- **Verify:** Re-render; exactly one canonical; target resolves `200`.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/canonicalization/canonical-placement-and-format.md`, `knowledge/canonicalization/common-canonical-mistakes.md`, `knowledge/canonicalization/canonical-is-a-hint.md`, `knowledge/canonicalization/cross-domain-canonical.md`, `knowledge/canonicalization/canonical-hijacking-and-copycat-content.md`

### X2 — robots.txt directives · Critical · C
- **Failure:** `Disallow:` blocks important paths (or `Disallow: /` on a live site) for Googlebot /
  Bingbot; or JS/CSS needed to render is blocked; or no `Sitemap:` directive. Remember `Disallow`
  stops *crawling*, never *indexing* — a disallowed URL can still be indexed URL-only, and its
  `noindex` is never seen (self-cancelling; see X3).
- **Probe:** Fetch `/robots.txt`; parse per-user-agent groups (single most-specific UA group wins);
  resolve Allow/Disallow by longest matching path (least-restrictive on ties), honoring only `*`/`$`
  wildcards; simulate whether key URLs are crawlable; confirm a `Sitemap:` line.
- **Fix:** **C** — propose the minimal diff; confirm. A blocked admin/cart path is likely a
  **decision**, not a defect — confirm intent.
- **Verify:** Re-fetch; important paths crawlable; `Sitemap:` present.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/crawlability/robots-txt-semantics.md`, `knowledge/robots-ai-crawlers/robots-txt-spec-rfc9309.md`

### X3 — noindex leakage (`meta robots` / `X-Robots-Tag`) · Critical · C
- **Failure:** A page that should rank carries `noindex`/`nofollow` in a `<meta name="robots">` or the
  `X-Robots-Tag` response header (classic staging→prod leak). Silently de-indexes. The header is the
  only mechanism for non-HTML files, so a probe that reads only the HTML head misses header-level
  leaks. A `Disallow`ed page can never *see* its `noindex` — robots.txt block + noindex is
  self-defeating and can leave the URL indexed.
- **Probe:** Parse both the meta tag **and** the response header on every page; flag `noindex` on any
  page that belongs in the index. Cross-check that any intended `noindex` page is actually crawlable
  (not also `Disallow`ed). (Missing `noindex` on a thin utility page is the inverse — Low.) Note
  `noarchive` is now a historical no-op on Google — never flag its absence.
- **Fix:** **C** — confirm removal. A `noindex` on staging / thank-you / filter pages is a
  **decision** — confirm intent, then record it in `decisions.md`.
- **Verify:** Re-render + re-fetch header; no unintended `noindex`.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/indexability/noindex-two-mechanisms.md`, `knowledge/indexability/noindex-requires-crawlable.md`, `knowledge/indexability/serving-directives-catalog.md`, `knowledge/metadata/robots-meta-directives.md`

### X4 — Redirects & status codes · High (Critical on money/nav paths) · C
- **Failure:** Internal links to 404/410/5xx; permanent moves served as 302 (weak) not 301 (strong);
  long redirect chains (**1 hop is ideal; Googlebot follows ~10 hops before returning a redirect
  error**); redirect loops; a redirect that lands on a 404. A permanent move via `meta refresh`
  instead of a server-side 301 is weaker still.
- **Probe:** Resolve each internal link and each redirect target to its final status; count hops (flag
  chains, keep it to 1 where you can); detect loops. Read each status per Googlebot's meaning — 200
  index-eligible, 301/308 strong canonical signal, 302/303/307 weak, 404/410 drop, 5xx/429 slow then
  drop. (Tier 2 for live; Tier 0 for configured redirect rules.)
- **Fix:** Normalizing an **internal link** to its final URL (killing a chain) is Tier **A**. Creating
  or editing **redirect rules** (`_redirects`/`.htaccess`/`vercel.json`/`next.config`) is Tier **C**.
- **Verify:** Every touched target resolves to a single `200`.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/urls-redirects/status-code-seo-meaning.md`, `knowledge/metadata/meta-refresh-vs-301.md`, `knowledge/foundations/thresholds.md`

### X5 — hreflang reciprocity · High (affected markets) · C  *(skip cleanly if single-locale)*
- **Failure:** Multilingual site with non-reciprocal return tags, a missing self-referencing tag,
  no `x-default`, invalid ISO 639-1 / 3166-1 codes, or `hreflang` targets that don't match canonicals.
  A missing return tag makes Google ignore the whole annotation; canonicalizing all locales to one
  master URL collapses the cluster. Mis-clusters the wrong-language page into markets.
- **Probe:** For each locale cluster, confirm every page lists every alternate **and** itself, an
  `x-default` exists, codes are valid, delivery uses exactly one method (head link / HTTP header /
  sitemap — never mixed), and each locale self-canonicals to a `200` matching its hreflang target.
- **Fix:** **C** — hreflang cluster edits touch indexation; propose the full corrected cluster; confirm.
- **Verify:** Re-render; reciprocity holds across the cluster; targets `200`.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/hreflang/hreflang-reciprocity.md`, `knowledge/hreflang/hreflang-canonical.md`, `knowledge/hreflang/hreflang-common-errors.md`

### X6 — Duplicate host / scheme consolidation · High · C
- **Failure:** `www` vs non-`www`, `http` vs `https`, trailing-slash, `index.html`, or tracking-param
  variants all return `200` without consolidating — every variant is a distinct URL to Google, so
  duplicate content dilutes ranking. HTTPS is preferred as canonical over an HTTP duplicate.
- **Probe:** Request the known variants; confirm one canonical host+scheme+slash-policy, others `301`
  to it.
- **Fix:** **C** — host/scheme canonicalization is the highest-blast-radius edit; confirm. The chosen
  canonical host is often a **decision** — confirm before changing. Use a 301 to retire a duplicate
  URL, `rel="canonical"` to consolidate while both stay reachable, `noindex` only to drop from the
  index (never for consolidation).
- **Verify:** Variants `301` to the single canonical origin.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/urls-redirects/canonical-host-consolidation.md`, `knowledge/urls-redirects/https-signal.md`, `knowledge/canonicalization/canonical-vs-301-vs-noindex.md`

### X7 — XML sitemap validity · High (Medium if merely polluted) · C
- **Failure:** Sitemap is invalid XML, lists non-200 / `noindex` / redirected / non-canonical URLs,
  lacks `<lastmod>` (or carries an inaccurate one), exceeds 50,000 URLs or 50 MB uncompressed, or
  isn't referenced in `robots.txt`. `<changefreq>`/`<priority>` are ignored outright.
- **Probe:** Fetch and parse; sample `<loc>` entries for status + canonical + indexability; check
  counts against the 50k/50 MB caps and the `robots.txt` `Sitemap:` reference. `<lastmod>` is used
  only if consistently and verifiably accurate. Note: the HTTP sitemap ping is dead (Google 404 /
  Bing 410 since 2023) — discover via the `robots.txt` `Sitemap:` directive + Search Console, use
  IndexNow for Bing.
- **Fix:** Regenerating the sitemap is Tier **C** (it steers what Google crawls) — confirm; prefer
  fixing the generator config over hand-editing.
- **Verify:** Valid XML; every `<loc>` is a 200 canonical indexable URL with an accurate `<lastmod>`.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/sitemaps/sitemap-url-eligibility.md`, `knowledge/sitemaps/sitemap-lastmod-and-ignored-tags.md`, `knowledge/sitemaps/sitemap-discovery-and-ping-deprecation.md`, `knowledge/sitemaps/sitemap-location-and-index-format.md`, `knowledge/foundations/thresholds.md`

---

## Phase C — Crawlability

### C1 — Orphan pages & crawl depth · Medium · A/flag
- **Failure:** Important pages are unreachable from internal links or sit deeper than ~3 clicks.
  Google's primary discovery is following links — an orphan (zero inbound internal links) relies on
  sitemaps/external links and may never be found.
- **Probe:** Build the internal link graph from the homepage; flag pages in the sitemap/routing not
  reachable ≤3 clicks, and pages with zero inbound internal links.
- **Fix:** Adding a contextual internal link from a relevant existing page is Tier **A** (uses real
  routes only). Architecture changes are **flag**.
- **Verify:** Page now reachable ≤3 clicks.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/crawlability/orphan-discoverability.md`, `knowledge/architecture/site-structure-depth.md`, `knowledge/architecture/internal-linking.md`

### C2 — Soft-404s · Medium · flag
- **Failure:** "Not found" / empty pages return `200` instead of `404`/`410`, wasting crawl budget and
  polluting the index (GSC labels these "Soft 404").
- **Probe:** Request known-missing routes; flag any that return `200` with empty/error content. Read
  the status per Googlebot's meaning (404/410 drop the URL; a 200 passes content forward).
- **Fix:** **Flag** — correct status handling is framework config, usually a decision to route.
- **Verify:** Missing routes return `404`/`410`.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/indexability/index-coverage-states.md`, `knowledge/urls-redirects/status-code-seo-meaning.md`

### C3 — Crawl traps & index bloat · Medium · flag
- **Failure:** Infinite faceted/filter/param or calendar/pagination URL spaces burn crawl budget; a
  large tail of thin/duplicate/auto-generated URLs gets "crawled-not-indexed", diluting quality
  signals. Crawl budget only matters at scale (~1M+ pages, ~10k+/day, or lots of "Discovered - not
  indexed"); 5xx/429 throttle crawling, 4xx drop URLs.
- **Probe:** Inspect faceted/param URL patterns and pagination for unbounded permutations; look for
  index bloat.
- **Fix:** **Flag** with a recommendation — block trap URLs via `robots.txt` param `Disallow` or
  `#`-fragments (NOT `noindex`, since disallowed pages are never crawled to see it); consolidate or
  `noindex` (crawl-allowed) genuine thin duplicates, never blanket-noindex.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/crawlability/crawl-traps.md`, `knowledge/crawlability/crawl-budget-and-server-health.md`, `knowledge/indexability/index-bloat-pruning.md`

### C4 — AI-crawler access · Medium (High if a citation bot is blocked) · flag (decision, never auto-fix)
- **Failure:** `robots.txt` blocks a **search/retrieval** bot (`OAI-SearchBot`, `PerplexityBot`,
  `Claude-SearchBot`, `Bingbot`) or `Googlebot` on indexable routes — which removes AI-answer / AI-Overview
  citation eligibility (surface at **High** urgency). A block on a **training-only** bot (`GPTBot`,
  `ClaudeBot`, `CCBot`, `Bytespider`, `Amazonbot`) is a consent/licensing **decision**, not a defect.
  `Google-Extended` / `Applebot-Extended` are opt-out **control tokens** that issue zero requests and
  never appear in logs — blocking them does NOT cut off AI answers or touch ranking; do not report
  them as an AEO block. Note robots.txt is voluntary — user-triggered agents may ignore it and some
  crawlers evade blocks entirely (real enforcement is IP/WAF/edge, not a `Disallow` line).
- **Probe:** Parse `robots.txt` per user-agent and classify each blocked agent into its tier before flagging.
- **Fix:** **Flag only**, never auto-unblock — allow/deny is a business/legal choice; surface the tier and
  its citation cost, confirm intent, and record the decision in `claude/seo/decisions.md`.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/answer-engines/ai-crawlers.md`, `knowledge/robots-ai-crawlers/ai-crawler-ua-catalog-2026.md`, `knowledge/robots-ai-crawlers/ai-training-optout-tokens.md`, `knowledge/robots-ai-crawlers/ai-crawler-compliance-reality.md`

---

## Phase R — Rendering & mobile

### R1 — Content in the initial HTML (render parity) · High · flag
- **Failure:** Critical content/links exist only after client-side JS hydration. Googlebot crawls the
  raw HTTP response first then defer-renders in a separate queue (the render can lag), and **AI
  crawlers don't run JS at all** — so CSR-only content is weak for indexing and invisible to answer
  engines. CSR ships an empty shell (worst); SSR/SSG/ISR ship content-complete HTML (safe).
- **Probe:** Compare initial server HTML to the rendered DOM — confirm title/H1/canonical/robots/
  hreflang/primary-content/internal-links survive into the rendered HTML. Tier-0 heuristic when no
  renderer: near-empty `<body>` + large JS bundle + `<div id="root|app">` + no SSR/SSG/prerender
  config → raise. Never treat "Tier 1 unavailable" as "pass". Keep critical markup high — Googlebot
  fetches only the **first 2 MB of HTML** (2026).
- **Fix:** **Flag** — SSR/SSG/prerender is an architecture decision; recommend, don't rewrite. Do not
  recommend dynamic rendering (user-agent-split prerendering) — Google calls it a workaround, not a
  durable fix.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/js-rendering/render-parity.md`, `knowledge/js-rendering/framework-rendering-modes.md`, `knowledge/js-rendering/rendering-pipeline.md`, `knowledge/js-rendering/dynamic-rendering-deprecated.md`, `knowledge/crawlability/googlebot-fetch-behavior.md`

### R2 — Mobile-first parity · High · flag
- **Failure:** Mobile serves less content, fewer links, or different structured data than desktop —
  Google indexes the **mobile** version exclusively, so desktop-only text/links/JSON-LD/metadata is
  effectively unindexed.
- **Probe:** Compare mobile vs desktop rendered content/links/JSON-LD (Tier 1).
- **Fix:** **Flag** — parity is a template/design fix; recommend responsive web design (one URL, one
  HTML source), which structurally sidesteps parity gaps.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/mobile/mobile-first-parity.md`, `knowledge/mobile/responsive-recommended-pattern.md`

### R3 — Viewport & charset meta · High · A
- **Failure:** No `<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">` (breaks mobile
  rendering and mobile-first indexing); or no `<meta charset="utf-8">` as an early `<head>` child — a
  mis-declared/undeclared encoding produces mojibake that corrupts both indexing and AI extraction.
- **Probe:** Check the rendered `<head>` on every template for both tags (plus a correct `<html lang>`
  / `dir`); confirm `charset` is within the first ~1KB of `<head>` and the viewport still allows zoom.
  Do **not** flag as missing the tags Google ignores — `meta keywords`/`author`/`rating` and the
  deprecated sitelinks-searchbox markup.
- **Fix:** **A** — add the missing tag via the head mechanism (`<meta charset="utf-8">` as the first
  `<head>` child).
- **Verify:** Both present in the rendered head; `charset` is utf-8 and early.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/metadata/document-declaration-meta.md`, `knowledge/metadata/dead-meta-tags.md`

### R4 — Tap targets & legibility · Low · flag
- **Failure:** Tap targets too small/tightly spaced or base font too small for mobile. The px
  thresholds are accessibility-derived (Lighthouse/WCAG), not published Google ranking cutoffs — treat
  as guidance.
- **Probe:** Tier 1 rendered inspection.
- **Fix:** **Flag** (CSS/design).
- *Depth:* `knowledge/mobile/mobile-usability-signals.md`

---

## Phase O — On-page & semantic HTML

The largest volume of safe, high-value fixes. Never overwrite non-empty human copy — fill gaps and
repair markup; propose (Tier B) rewrites of existing copy.

### O1 — Title tag · High · A (add) / B (rewrite)
- **Failure:** Missing, empty, duplicated sitewide, or generic `<title>`. The primary ranking anchor
  and the SERP click target — and Google rewrites the title link when it is empty, stale, inaccurate,
  boilerplate, or duplicative.
- **Probe:** Extract the rendered `<title>` from every page; flag missing/empty/duplicate/generic.
  Front-load the keyword; ~pixel-width (~600px) is guidance, not a hard rule — **do not obsess over
  exact character counts** (fix the *quality* cause of a rewrite, not the length).
- **Fix:** **A** to add a missing title derived from the page's own H1/content. A rewrite of existing
  copy is **B** — propose the text.
- **Verify:** One non-empty, unique title in the rendered head.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/titles-descriptions/title-uniqueness-duplication.md`, `knowledge/titles-descriptions/title-link-generation.md`, `knowledge/foundations/thresholds.md`

### O2 — Meta description · Medium · B
- **Failure:** Missing or duplicated `<meta name="description">`. Not a ranking factor, but a CTR /
  snippet lever and the AI-preview snippet — Google builds the snippet mostly from body content and
  only uses the description when it describes the page better.
- **Probe:** Extract from every page; flag missing/duplicate. Aim for a snippet-length summary
  (pixel-width, not an exact character count).
- **Fix:** **B** — generate **only** by summarizing the page's own visible body ("use only facts on
  the page; invent no price/shipping/feature claims"); ungroundable → `[[NEEDS: ...]]`. Propose the
  exact text.
- **Verify:** Present, unique, grounded in visible content.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/titles-descriptions/meta-description-snippet-role.md`, `knowledge/foundations/thresholds.md`

### O3 — Headings & H1 · Medium · A/B
- **Failure:** No H1, multiple H1s, skipped levels (H1→H3), or non-descriptive headings — weakens the
  heading tree that answer engines chunk on for passage extraction.
- **Probe:** Extract the heading tree per page; confirm exactly one descriptive H1 and no skipped
  levels.
- **Fix:** Correcting a heading **level** (structural) is **A**; rewriting heading **text** is **B**.
- **Verify:** One H1, contiguous hierarchy.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/answer-engines/extractability.md`

### O4 — Semantic landmarks · Low-Medium · A
- **Failure:** Div-soup instead of `<main>`/`<nav>`/`<article>`/`<header>`/`<footer>` — weakens
  machine parsing and answer-engine chunking.
- **Probe:** Check for real landmark elements on each template.
- **Fix:** **A** where a wrapper maps 1:1 to a landmark; otherwise **flag**.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/answer-engines/extractability.md`

### O5 — `<html lang>` · Low-Medium · A
- **Failure:** Missing or wrong `lang` attribute on `<html>` (and a missing `dir` on RTL locales).
- **Probe:** Check the rendered root element.
- **Fix:** **A** — set from the site's primary locale (Phase 0).
- **Verify:** Correct `lang` present.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/metadata/document-declaration-meta.md`

### O6 — Internal linking & anchor text · Medium · A
- **Failure:** Key pages under-linked; non-descriptive anchors ("click here"); the same exact anchor
  repeated; broken internal links; pagination via non-crawlable controls. `rel="next"/"prev"` is dead
  (unused since 2019) — paginate with crawlable `<a href>` links and a unique self-canonical per page.
- **Probe:** Build the link graph (Google only follows `<a href>`); flag generic/duplicate anchors and
  404-ing internal links; use **real, existing routes only** — never invent a link target. Consider
  pillar-and-cluster topology for topical authority.
- **Fix:** **A** — add a contextual link with descriptive, varied anchor text to a real page; repoint
  a broken link to the correct existing route.
- **Verify:** Anchor descriptive; target resolves `200`.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/architecture/internal-linking.md`, `knowledge/architecture/pagination.md`, `knowledge/architecture/pillar-cluster.md`

### O7 — URL structure · Low-Medium · flag
- **Failure:** Uppercase (URLs are case-sensitive), underscores instead of hyphens, session IDs, deep
  nesting, inconsistent trailing-slash policy.
- **Probe:** Inspect the URL set for these patterns.
- **Fix:** **Flag** — changing live URLs needs redirects (de-index risk); recommend for new URLs only.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/urls-redirects/url-design.md`

---

## Phase M — Media

### M1 — Image alt text · Medium (bulk) / Low (single) · A/B
- **Failure:** Contentful images missing `alt`; or decorative images with noisy `alt`.
- **Probe:** List `<img>` per page; flag missing `alt` on contentful images and non-empty `alt` on
  clearly decorative ones.
- **Fix:** `alt=""` on decorative images is **A**. Descriptive `alt` on a contentful image is **B** —
  generate from the actual image ("describe only what is clearly visible; do not guess"); ungroundable
  → `[[NEEDS: ...]]`. Never overwrite existing meaningful alt.
- **Verify:** Every contentful image has grounded alt; decorative images have `alt=""`.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/media/alt-text.md`

### M2 — Explicit image dimensions (CLS) · Medium · A
- **Failure:** `<img>` without `width`/`height` (or CSS `aspect-ratio`) causes layout shift.
- **Probe:** Flag contentful images lacking intrinsic dimensions; read the real file to get them.
- **Fix:** **A** — add `width`/`height` from the file.
- **Verify:** Dimensions present; re-check CLS if Tier 1 available.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/media/image-perf.md`

### M3 — Next-gen format & sizing · Medium · flag
- **Failure:** Large JPEG/PNG where WebP/AVIF would cut bytes; oversized images.
- **Probe:** Compare rendered dimensions to intrinsic size and format.
- **Fix:** **Flag** (asset pipeline) with the recommendation.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/media/image-perf.md`

### M4 — Lazy-loading below the fold · Low · A
- **Failure:** Below-fold images eagerly loaded (slows LCP); **or** the LCP image lazy-loaded (delays
  it — the inverse mistake).
- **Probe:** Determine fold position (Tier 1) or use document order as a heuristic.
- **Fix:** **A** — `loading="lazy"` below the fold; ensure the LCP image is **not** lazy.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/media/image-perf.md`

### M5 — Responsive `srcset` · Low · flag
- **Failure:** One fixed image served to all viewports.
- **Fix:** **Flag** with a `srcset`/`sizes` recommendation.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/media/image-perf.md`

### M6 — Media discoverability · Low · flag
- **Failure:** Images/videos are hard for Google to find or interpret — CDN/other-domain images with
  no image sitemap, opaque filenames (`IMG_1234.jpg`) instead of short descriptive hyphenated ones, or
  video on a non-indexable watch page / without a fetchable streaming URL, stable thumbnail, or key
  moments.
- **Probe:** Where images live off-domain, check for an image sitemap (image `<loc>` may be
  cross-domain); sample filenames and surrounding text; for video, confirm an indexable watch page +
  fetchable streaming file + valid stable thumbnail. (`VideoObject`/image structured data is D1.)
- **Fix:** **Flag** — image sitemaps and filename conventions are pipeline/config; recommend.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/media/image-sitemap.md`, `knowledge/media/video-seo.md`, `knowledge/sitemaps/sitemap-media-and-extensions.md`

---

## Phase D — Structured data (Schema.org / JSON-LD)

Validity and content-match matter more than volume. Emit only schema that maps to **visible** content.

### D1 — Coverage by template · Medium · A/B
- **Failure:** Eligible rich-result markup absent: sitewide `Organization` + `WebSite`,
  `BreadcrumbList` on all, `Article`/`BlogPosting` on posts, `Product`+`Offer`(+`AggregateRating` only
  if real) on products, `LocalBusiness`+NAP on local, plus `VideoObject`/`Event`/`Recipe` where that
  content exists. Emit only types Google still renders — **do not** add `FAQPage`/`HowTo` expecting a
  rich result (removed; see D5).
- **Probe:** Per template, list present `@type`s vs. the content actually on the page.
- **Fix:** **A** where every field maps 1:1 to visible content (breadcrumbs from real nav, `headline`
  = visible H1, `datePublished` = existing frontmatter; `Article` has no required props). Any
  **fact-bearing** field (price, availability, rating, review) is Tier **C** — never fabricated,
  confirm. Don't hand-add schema the project already emits (check Phase 0 first).
- **Verify:** Valid JSON-LD; values equal visible content.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/structured-data/rich-results-eligibility-2026.md`, `knowledge/structured-data/article-schema-recipe.md`, `knowledge/structured-data/breadcrumb-organization-schema-recipe.md`, `knowledge/structured-data/product-offer-review-schema-recipe.md`, `knowledge/structured-data/event-schema-recipe.md`, `knowledge/structured-data/recipe-video-schema-recipe.md`

### D2 — Validity · Medium · A
- **Failure:** JSON-LD doesn't parse, or a declared `@type` is missing required/recommended props
  (e.g. `Offer` needs `price`+`priceCurrency`; `AggregateRating` needs `ratingValue`+count; `Event`
  needs `name`+`startDate`+`location`).
- **Probe:** Extract each `application/ld+json` block; `JSON.parse` it (`node -e` if present); check
  required + recommended props against the type's recipe. Use the **Rich Results Test** for Google
  rich-result *eligibility* and the **Schema Markup Validator** for generic schema.org validity — they
  answer different questions and the Rich Results Test has no public API, so append "verify eligibility
  at search.google.com/test/rich-results".
- **Fix:** **A** — repair malformed JSON; add missing required props **only** if their value is on the
  page, else `[[NEEDS: ...]]`. **A** also covers FORMAT-LITERAL normalization that preserves the value:
  expand enum shorthand to its full schema.org IRI (`InStock` → `https://schema.org/InStock`), coerce
  dates to ISO-8601, and make `url`/`image`/`logo`/`@id` absolute via the Phase-0 base-URL config —
  Google requires these serializations for rich-result eligibility, so a valid-but-shorthand block
  silently fails. Any change to the asserted **fact** (availability, price, rating) stays Tier **C**.
- **Verify:** Parses; required props present; enum values are full schema.org IRIs, dates are ISO-8601,
  URLs are absolute.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/structured-data/product-offer-review-schema-recipe.md`, `knowledge/measurement-tooling/structured-data-validators.md`

### D3 — Content-match (spam guard) · High · flag/C
- **Failure:** Markup describes content not visible on the page (hidden/absent reviews, fake ratings,
  FAQ not shown) — a Google structured-data spam-policy violation that risks a manual action removing
  rich-result eligibility.
- **Probe:** Diff each JSON-LD value against visible content.
- **Fix:** **C**/flag — remove or correct mismatched markup; confirm. **Never** introduce
  fact-bearing markup not shown on the page.
- **Verify:** Every markup value appears in the rendered page.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/structured-data/structured-data-visible-content-policy.md`

### D4 — Entity graph & `sameAs` · Low-Medium · A
- **Failure:** `Organization` lacks `sameAs` links (Wikipedia/Wikidata/LinkedIn/Crunchbase) and `@id`
  connections — weakens Knowledge-Graph and AI-citation signals. (`Organization` has no *required*
  props but should carry `name`/`url`/`logo`.)
- **Probe:** Check `Organization`/`WebSite` for `sameAs`, `logo`, and `@id` cross-references.
- **Fix:** **A** using **known, real** profiles (from the site) only; otherwise flag.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/structured-data/breadcrumb-organization-schema-recipe.md`, `knowledge/answer-engines/entity-signals.md`

### D5 — Deprecation awareness · Info · (do not flag as missing)
- **Note:** **FAQ and HowTo rich results are FULLY REMOVED from Google Search (FAQ removed
  2026-05-07; HowTo earlier), plus a June-2025 batch.** Keep existing FAQ/HowTo markup for
  semantic/AI value if it maps to visible content, but **never flag their absence as a lost rich
  result** and **never promise** a rich result Google no longer renders. Emit only types Google still
  renders.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/structured-data/rich-results-eligibility-2026.md`, `knowledge/foundations/2026-seo-myths.md`

---

## Phase S — Social / discovery cards

### S1 — Open Graph · Low-Medium · A/B
- **Failure:** Missing `og:title`/`og:description`/`og:image`/`og:url`/`og:type` (the four required are
  `og:title`/`og:type`/`og:image`/`og:url`) — poor shares and AI previews; `og:image` should carry
  `og:image:alt`.
- **Probe:** Check OG tags per template.
- **Fix:** **A** — propagate `og:title`/`description` from the existing title/description and
  `og:image` from the page's hero (one 1200×630 ≈1.91:1 JPG/PNG at an absolute HTTPS URL works across
  Facebook/X/LinkedIn/Slack/Discord/WhatsApp/iMessage). A generated value is **B**.
- **Verify:** Complete OG set; `og:image` resolves `200`. Each platform bot GETs the URL and caches by
  URL with no JS — after changing tags, cache-bust via the platform's debugger.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/social/open-graph.md`, `knowledge/social/og-image-specs.md`, `knowledge/social/preview-caching.md`

### S2 — Twitter/X card · Low · A
- **Failure:** No `twitter:card` (X falls back to `og:*` for content, but needs one `twitter:card` to
  pick the layout).
- **Fix:** **A** — add `summary_large_image` on top of good OG.
- **Verify:** Card tags present.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/social/twitter-cards.md`

---

## Phase H — Internationalization  *(skip cleanly if single-locale)*

Reciprocity/cluster edits live in **X5** (de-index class). Here: presence and correctness advisories.

### H1 — Locale coverage & `x-default` · Medium · flag/C
- **Failure:** Some locales lack alternates; no `x-default` for the language selector / fallback;
  invalid ISO codes or a mixed delivery method. An hreflang annotation is
  `rel="alternate" hreflang="<code>" href="<url>"` delivered by exactly one method (head link / HTTP
  header / sitemap — hreflang alternates can ride in the sitemap).
- **Probe:** Map the locale graph; find gaps and code/format errors.
- **Fix:** Cluster edits are **C** (see X5). Advisory otherwise.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/hreflang/hreflang-syntax.md`, `knowledge/hreflang/hreflang-common-errors.md`

---

## Phase L — Local business  *(skip cleanly if not a physical-location business)*

Most local ranking lives **off-site** (Google Business Profile, reviews, citations), not in the repo.
The on-site job is consistent NAP, `LocalBusiness` markup, and genuinely-distinct location pages.

### L1 — Local business signals · Medium (local sites) · A/flag
- **Failure:** A physical-location business with inconsistent NAP across the site, no `LocalBusiness`
  schema tying NAP/geo/hours to the entity, city-swap template pages for multiple locations (thin /
  duplicate — filtered), or (off-site) an unclaimed Google Business Profile. The local pack ranks on
  relevance + distance + prominence, and the claimed GBP is the primary lever — off the site.
- **Probe:** Detect physical-presence signals (address/phone/map, "visit us"). **If none, document a
  one-line N/A skip.** Else check byte-consistent NAP (footer/contact/location pages), `LocalBusiness`
  markup, and one uniquely-written indexable page per location with its own NAP/hours/schema.
- **Fix:** **Flag** — GBP and third-party citations are off-site business assets, not repo edits.
  Wiring **real** NAP into `LocalBusiness` schema that matches visible content is Tier **A**; a
  fact-bearing field with no visible source stays **C**. Never fabricate an address, phone, or hours.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/local/gbp-local-pack.md`, `knowledge/local/nap-citations.md`, `knowledge/local/localbusiness-schema-signal.md`, `knowledge/local/multi-location-pages.md`, `knowledge/local/service-area-business-address.md`

---

## Phase P — Core Web Vitals & page experience

Delegate measurement to the installed `web-perf` skill when present; otherwise degrade. The official
pass/fail is the **75th-percentile of real-user FIELD data (CrUX, 28-day rolling)** — lab
(Lighthouse/PSI) is directional only. On low-traffic sites there is no field data; report lab results
as **"lab-only, not field-confirmed"** and never declare a green field pass from a lab run. Page
experience (of which CWV is one part) is a light, tie-breaker-class signal — relevance and helpful
content win first.

**For AEO:** CWV is *correlational*, not a confirmed citation factor — the mechanism is that AI crawlers
run on ~1-5s timeouts, so slow raw HTML risks being skipped. Report perf with that rationale; never state
CWV as a causal citation lever. *Depth:* `knowledge/answer-engines/ai-referral-measurement.md`

### P1 — LCP ≤ 2.5s · High · flag
- **Failure:** Largest Contentful Paint > 2.5s at p75 (needs-improvement 2.5-4s; poor > 4s). Common
  causes: LCP image lazy-loaded or not preloaded, slow TTFB, render-blocking CSS/JS.
- **Probe:** CrUX field where available; else Lighthouse/PSI lab, labeled directional.
- **Fix:** **Flag** with the specific cause + remedy (preload the LCP image, cut render-blocking).
- *Depth:* `knowledge/core-web-vitals/ttfb-lcp-input.md`, `knowledge/core-web-vitals/field-vs-lab-crux.md`, `knowledge/core-web-vitals/lcp-subparts-diagnosis.md`, `knowledge/core-web-vitals/page-experience-signal.md`, `knowledge/foundations/thresholds.md`

### P2 — INP ≤ 200ms · Medium-High · flag
- **Failure:** Interaction to Next Paint > 200ms at p75 (**INP replaced FID on 2024-03-12; FID is
  retired**). Causes: long tasks, heavy hydration, expensive handlers.
- **Fix:** **Flag** with the cause.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/core-web-vitals/inp-replaced-fid.md`, `knowledge/foundations/thresholds.md`

### P3 — CLS ≤ 0.1 · Medium-High · A(partial)/flag
- **Failure:** Cumulative Layout Shift > 0.1 at p75. Causes: images/embeds without reserved
  dimensions, injected banners, font-swap reflow.
- **Probe:** Tier 1 measurement; the image-dimension slice overlaps **M2**.
- **Fix:** Adding image dimensions is **A** (see M2); other causes **flag**.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/media/image-perf.md`, `knowledge/foundations/thresholds.md`

### P4 — Delivery hygiene · Medium · flag
- **Failure:** TTFB > 0.8s (p75; gates LCP, not itself a CWV); no gzip/brotli; render-blocking CSS/JS;
  unminified/unused CSS/JS; no `font-display: swap` / preload / subset; poor cache-control; no CDN.
- **Probe:** Response headers (Tier 0/2) + Lighthouse (Tier 1); PSI field vs lab labeled accordingly.
- **Fix:** **Flag** with prioritized remedies.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/core-web-vitals/ttfb-lcp-input.md`, `knowledge/measurement-tooling/pagespeed-field-vs-lab.md`

### P5 — Intrusive interstitials · Medium · flag
- **Failure:** Mobile popups blocking main content on arrival from Search (legal, login-gate, and
  small dismissible banners are exempt).
- **Fix:** **Flag**.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/mobile/intrusive-interstitials.md`

---

## Phase E — Content quality & E-E-A-T

Largely **flag-and-advise**, not silent edits — the skill can add bylines/dates/schema but must
**never fabricate** credentials or experience. Trust is the dominant axis (Sept 2025 rater guidelines
expanded YMYL scrutiny).

### E1 — Trust signals · High (YMYL) / Medium · flag
- **Failure:** No clear ownership/contact/about; YMYL pages (health/finance/legal/civic) without
  qualified, shown authorship or review; no HTTPS (a lightweight ranking signal folded into page
  experience). E-E-A-T is a rater rubric approximated by core signals, with Trust at the center.
- **Probe:** Check for contact/about, author attribution, HTTPS; classify YMYL templates.
- **Fix:** **Flag** — recommend; never invent credentials.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/content-eeat/eeat-framework.md`, `knowledge/urls-redirects/https-signal.md`

### E2 — Experience & Expertise · Medium · A(scaffold)/flag
- **Failure:** No author byline/bio/credentials; stock rather than original media; no first-hand
  signals. Bylines/credentials are E-E-A-T evidence for raters (heavily weighted on YMYL), not a
  mechanical bonus.
- **Fix:** Wiring an **existing** author name into a byline/`author` schema is **A**; writing bios or
  claiming experience is **flag** (human-authored).
- *Depth:* `knowledge/content-eeat/eeat-framework.md`, `knowledge/content-eeat/freshness-and-authorship.md`

### E3 — Helpful, people-first content · High · flag
- **Failure:** Thin, scaled, or mass-AI-generated boilerplate; keyword stuffing; doorway pages;
  duplicate/near-duplicate pages; keyword cannibalization (two pages targeting one query). Google
  judges by quality/purpose, not by whether AI produced it — mass low-value scaled output is the spam
  line (the "Helpful Content" system folded into core ranking Mar 2024).
- **Probe:** Detect thin/duplicate pages and cannibalization across the corpus; self-assess with
  Who / How / Why.
- **Fix:** **Flag** with consolidation/pruning advice.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/content-eeat/helpful-people-first-content.md`, `knowledge/content-eeat/scaled-content-site-reputation-spam.md`, `knowledge/content-eeat/ai-content-quality-not-origin.md`

### E4 — Freshness · Low-Medium · A
- **Failure:** Missing/inaccurate `dateModified`; stale load-bearing facts. Freshness helps only for
  recency-sensitive queries and only when content actually changed.
- **Fix:** Setting an accurate `dateModified` from **real edit metadata** is **A**; content updates are
  **flag**. **Never** deceptively bump a `dateModified` or a visible "last updated" date the content did
  not earn — it misleads users, backfires, and risks demotion; the only safe date action is syncing
  schema to the page's true committed/visible date, never "today".
- *Depth:* `knowledge/content-eeat/freshness-and-authorship.md`

---

## Phase G — Answer-engine optimization (GEO / AEO)

Get *quoted* by AI answer engines (AI Overviews, ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini, Claude, Copilot), not just
ranked. **Calibrated, not hyped** — see the Myths section. The biggest AEO wins are the *established*
technical ones (server-rendered content, valid visible-content-backed schema, AI-crawler access); the
content levers are recommend-only and **never fabricate** a stat, quote, date, or identity. Freshness is
covered by E4, schema validity/content-match by D2/D3, entity `sameAs` by D4, AI-crawler access by C4 —
this phase is the answer-engine-specific layer on top. Mostly **flag-and-advise**; a few structural fixes
are **A**/**B**.

Every AEO check is adjudicated and verified against the **raw/pre-JS HTML** — what AI crawlers actually
ingest (all except Gemini run zero JS) — never the hydrated DOM; a fix visible only in the rendered DOM
is a false AEO pass even when it is correct for Googlebot. **Label every AEO recommendation with its
evidence tier** — ESTABLISHED (server-rendered content, valid visible-content-backed schema, crawler
access) vs EMERGING/SPECULATIVE (the GEO-paper content-lever magnitudes, per-engine behavior) — and
phrase engine behavior as "reported/observed", never "the algorithm does X"; never state an EMERGING
magnitude as fact.

### G1 — Server-rendered answers · High · flag (shared with R1)
- **Failure:** The answer content exists only after client-side JS — and AI crawlers (except Gemini)
  don't run JS, so a CSR-only page is invisible to them. The single highest-impact AEO defect.
- **Probe:** From Phase 0's render surface, confirm the primary answer content + headings are in the
  **raw/initial HTML**, not just the hydrated DOM; the R1 heuristic applies when no renderer is available.
- **Fix:** **Flag** — SSR/SSG/prerender is an architecture decision; recommend, never auto-migrate.
- **Verify:** A no-JS fetch of the route contains the answer content in the response body.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/answer-engines/extractability.md`, `knowledge/js-rendering/framework-rendering-modes.md`

### G2 — Answer-first, standalone passages · Medium · flag (B if only reflowing existing content)
- **Failure:** Sections don't open with a direct 1-2 sentence answer; the answer is buried after
  background; passages open with dangling references ("this", "as above") that fail when quoted out of
  context; inherently tabular/enumerable content is rendered as dense prose; or a long / question-target
  page lacks a concise TL;DR / direct-answer block near the top.
- **Probe:** Chunk each page by `##`/`###` and read each chunk as if lifted alone: does it lead with the
  answer, stand on its own, and use a table/list/definition where the content is tabular/enumerable?
- **Fix:** **Flag** the restructure; a pure **reflow** of existing copy into a lead-answer / table / list
  / definition block (adding **no new facts**) is Tier **B** — propose the exact diff, never a silent
  rewrite, and preserve any heading anchor ids. Adding a top-of-page TL;DR that only summarizes facts
  already on the page (no new claims) is likewise Tier **B** — propose the exact text.
- **Verify:** Re-chunk — a lead answer exists and the reflow diff adds no new claims (content accuracy
  stays the author's judgment).
- *Depth:* `knowledge/answer-engines/extractability.md`

### G3 — Citeable patterns · Medium · flag
- **Failure:** No concrete statistics with named sources; no inline citations to credible sources (the
  single biggest peer-reviewed GEO lever); unsourced quantitative claims ("studies show"); no one-
  sentence "X is Y" definition of the core term; noun-fragment headings where a question fits; no
  named-expert quote where one genuinely applies.
- **Probe:** Scan for unsourced stats/claims, missing definitional leads, and bare-noun headings on
  question-target pages.
- **Fix:** **Flag** — content improvements; recommend and **never fabricate** a statistic, quote,
  citation URL, or source. A proposed question-shaped heading rewrite is Tier **B** and must preserve the
  old anchor id.
- **Verify:** N/A (the author supplies real sources); any proposed heading rewrite keeps anchors resolving.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/answer-engines/citeable-patterns.md`

### G4 — Entity & author signals · Low-Medium · A/flag
- **Failure:** `Organization`/`WebSite` lacks `sameAs` to real knowledge-graph nodes (overlaps D4);
  content pages have no author byline / `Person` schema; the brand name is spelled inconsistently in
  prose; no `/about` entity page.
- **Probe:** Check entity `sameAs`, a visible byline + `Person` schema on content templates, prose
  brand-name consistency, and an about page.
- **Fix:** Wiring **known, real** `sameAs`/author identity into schema is Tier **A**; **never fabricate**
  a name, credential, or `sameAs` URL (bios/credentials are human-authored — **flag**). Normalizing a
  brand-name variant in **prose only** (excluding code identifiers) is Tier **B**. A real FAQ mapping to
  visible content aids extraction, but FAQ rich results are gone — add for readers, never promise a rich
  result, never invent Q&A.
- **Verify:** Schema parses; every `sameAs` resolves 2xx and was user-supplied; prose uses one canonical
  brand spelling.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/answer-engines/entity-signals.md`, `knowledge/structured-data/breadcrumb-organization-schema-recipe.md`

### G5 — Per-engine citation readiness · Info · flag
- **Failure:** The audit reports on-page readiness without accounting for how engines differ or the
  off-site component of citation, so the owner over-expects on-page edits alone to win.
- **Probe:** Summarize per-engine sourcing (ChatGPT Wikipedia/Bing-leaning, Perplexity Reddit/YouTube-
  leaning, AI Overviews from the main Google index, Gemini renders JS, Copilot via Bing) and flag
  off-site gaps (Wikipedia/Wikidata, Reddit/G2).
- **Fix:** **Flag / advise only** — off-site presence and per-engine strategy are not repo edits; state
  that outcomes are probabilistic and never promise a citation.
- **Verify:** N/A (advisory).
- *Depth:* `knowledge/answer-engines/platform-behavior.md`

### G6 — AI-referral measurement · Info · A(config)/flag
- **Failure:** No way to see AI-assistant referral traffic, so AEO work can't be observed at all.
- **Probe:** Inspect the analytics config for an AI-source channel group / referrer regex over the AI
  host list (chatgpt.com, claude.ai, perplexity.ai, gemini.google.com, copilot.microsoft.com, …); note
  the losses (in-app ChatGPT has no referrer; AI-Overview clicks count as Google organic).
- **Fix:** Adding the channel-group/referrer config is Tier **A** where the analytics config is a
  code/file surface; it is instrumentation, not an on-page signal. Outcomes stay advisory.
- **Verify:** The channel group/regex is present in the config; traffic/citation **outcomes** are excluded
  from the verdict.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/answer-engines/ai-referral-measurement.md`

---

## Phase AC — Agentic-commerce readiness  *(skip cleanly if the site sells nothing)*

The newest, most speculative layer (`confidence: low`) — an emerging *transaction* channel where AI
agents buy on a user's behalf, not a ranking/citation surface. It is backend commerce plumbing, **not**
a markup gap: the agent transacts against a product feed + checkout API, not the page's HTML or its
Product/Offer JSON-LD (D1/D2), so nothing here is ever an auto-fix. Applies only to sites that actually
sell products — otherwise a one-line N/A skip.

### AC1 — Agentic checkout & payment-mandate readiness · Info (Low for e-commerce) · flag/C
- **Failure:** A product-selling site offers no path for AI agents to transact — no ACP (Agentic
  Commerce Protocol) product feed + checkout endpoints powering ChatGPT Instant Checkout, and no AP2
  (Agent Payments Protocol) signed-mandate support underneath — so agent-driven purchases route to
  competitors who expose one. The ACP feed is a **separate artifact** from on-page Product/Offer schema
  (complementary, never a substitute), and both protocols require a real payment/fulfillment backend.
- **Probe:** Only for sites that genuinely sell products (else document the one-line N/A skip). Check
  for a structured product feed and the 5 ACP checkout endpoints (`POST /checkout_sessions` plus
  `/{id}`, `/{id}/complete`, `/{id}/cancel`, and `GET /{id}`), and whether AP2's Intent/Cart/Payment
  Mandates (W3C Verifiable Credentials) are honored where the card network requires them. Treat on-page
  Product schema as a distinct check (D1/D2), never evidence of ACP coverage.
- **Fix:** **C / flag only** — adopting ACP/AP2 is a business + engineering decision needing a real
  payment/fulfillment backend, never an auto-fix or a markup change. Surface the opportunity, confirm
  intent, record in `claude/seo/decisions.md`. Everything here is emerging and probabilistic — never
  promise agent-driven sales.
- **Verify:** N/A (advisory); if adopted, the feed ingests and the checkout endpoints round-trip a
  session — adoption **outcomes** stay out of the verdict.
- *Depth:* `knowledge/agentic-commerce/acp-agentic-checkout.md`, `knowledge/agentic-commerce/ap2-agent-payments-protocol.md`

---

## Non-checks / Myths — do NOT flag these (they waste the owner's review budget)

Baked in from the maintainer's own SOTA guidance and confirmed by 2026 web research. The audit must
**not** report any of these as a defect:

- **`llms.txt`** — no major AI system uses it (Google confirmed). Do not score a site down for lacking
  it. Mention as an optional, zero-guarantee opportunity only if asked.
- **FAQPage / HowTo schema for rich results** — **fully removed from Google Search (FAQ 2026-05-07)**.
  Don't flag missing; **never promise** either rich result.
- **Word-count minimums** ("must be 1,500+ words") — not a ranking factor; adding words without
  structure gives ~0 AI-citation lift.
- **Exact character-count rules for titles/descriptions** — it's **pixel-width, not char count**;
  Google rewrites most titles, so front-load the payload instead of trimming to a magic number.
- **Keyword-density targets / stuffing** — measurably *worse* for both ranking and AI citation.
- **Hand-added JSON-LD as a GEO lever** — controlled tests show ~no citation uplift; emit the schema
  that earns rich results (which the project usually already does) and stop there.

### Anti-patterns the audit must never DO (not just "not flag")

- **Never deceptively bump a date** — do not touch a `dateModified` or a visible "last updated" date the
  content did not earn (E4 may only sync schema to the true committed/visible date).
- **Never cloak** — no `display:none` "AI-only" blocks, hidden keyword text, or model-directed
  instructions; same manual-action family as spammy markup. Anything touched stays equally visible and
  truthful to humans (detecting existing cloaking is a reportable finding, not something to add).
- **Never promise a citation or ranking** — state outcomes as probabilistic; phrase engine behavior as
  "reported/observed", never "the algorithm does X".
- **Never mass-block or mass-unblock AI crawlers** by default — it is a business/legal decision (see C4).

*Depth:* `knowledge/foundations/2026-seo-myths.md`
